title: Vera Rubin vs GB200: Inference TCO Analysis
summary: The article compares Nvidia&\#x27;s Vera Rubin NVL72 architecture with GB200 NVL72 for inference, revealing that Vera Rubin achieves 5.4x performance per megawatt and 5x performance per dollar on DeepSeek R1 through extreme co-design including 3-bit LUT Tensor Cores and SM140 Feynman GPU. This analysis underscores Nvidia&\#x27;s focus on inference cost-efficiency, crucial for scaling AI workloads. The architectural innovations may set new performance-per-watt and performance-per-dollar benchmarks for the industry. Vera Rubin NVL72 integrates a 3-bit lookup-table-based Tensor Core and SM140 Feynman GPU, along with Vera CPU, NVLink 6, ConnectX-9, BlueField-4, and Spectrum-6. It delivers 5.4x performance per MW and 5x performance per dollar over GB200 NVL72 for DeepSeek R1 inference.
context: Nvidia&\#x27;s rack-scale Oberon architecture evolves from GB200 \(based on Blackwell\) to Vera Rubin \(Rubin GPU + Vera CPU\). The Rubin GPU introduces 3-bit LUT Tensor Cores for efficient low-bit inference, and the upcoming Feynman GPU \(2028\) continues this trend. Such hardware-software co-design aims to dramatically reduce total cost of ownership for large-scale AI inference.
